fresh_facts.claim: U.S. and Israeli airstrikes targeted over 12,300 sites in Iran including weapons production, infrastructure, petrochemical zones, and the Bushehr nuclear plant as of April 5, 2026.
Iran shot down an F-15E fighter, A-10 Warthog, and MQ-9 Reaper aircraft; a U.S. aerial refueling tanker crashed in Iraq killing four crew members.
Israeli strikes hit petrochemical factories in Mahshahr killing at least five people and targeted a pharmaceutical plant.
IDF claims over 4,200 strikes neutralized 80% of Iran's air defenses.
U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated a 90% reduction in Iranian missile and drone attacks.
President Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face destruction of power plants.
The IRGC claimed advanced ballistic strikes on Diego Garcia base and vowed attacks on U.S. tech firms and Gulf targets.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes on February 28, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed as the new Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts following his father's death.
President Trump announced a 15-point U.S. peace proposal to Iran covering Strait of Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Tehran is reviewing the U.S. peace proposal but not formally negotiating.
Trump postponed strikes on Iranian energy sites for five days and instructed envoys to continue indirect phone discussions via Pakistan.
Iran agreed to release 20 cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz starting in early April 2026.
The IRGC took de facto control of the Iranian government amid a deepening power struggle following the Supreme Leader's death.
President Pezeshkian faced hardline backlash for a conditional war-end offer.
Hardliners branded ex-Foreign Minister Zarif a traitor for calling to end the war and urged his and ex-President Rouhani's arrest.
Iran appointed Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, a hardline ex-IRGC deputy, as Supreme National Security Council chief following Ali Larijani's death in an Israeli strike.
Iran faces severe medicine shortages with insulin prices at 70 million rials (approximately $46.70).
Iran experienced a record-long internet blackout per NetBlocks reports.
Two men were hanged on April 5, 2026 for storming a military site during January protests.
Two political prisoners and a Basij base fire detainee were executed in connection with ongoing unrest.
A U.S. airman was rescued from a downed F-15E aircraft in Iran.
Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and UAE intercepted Iranian drones and missiles during the ongoing conflict.
A guard was killed in the Bushehr nuclear plant strike with no radiation spike reported.
fresh_facts.source: Iranian state media and international reports

fresh_facts.claim: Intelligence indicates Iran maintains intact underground silos with thousands of remaining drones and missiles.
Trump threatened to seize Kharg Island oil or extract enriched uranium from Iran.
Trump warned of bombing Iran back to the Stone Age.

regime_fall.current_status: U.S. and Israeli forces have conducted over 12,300 strikes targeting Iranian military infrastructure, weapons production, and nuclear facilities, degrading air defenses by 80% while Iran maintains underground capabilities and underground missile stockpiles; diplomatically, Trump's 48-hour ultimatum on the Strait of Hormuz follows a rejected peace proposal, though indirect negotiations continue via Pakistan with mixed Iranian signals.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Comprehensive U.S.-Israeli aerial campaign struck over 12,300 Iranian targets on April 5, 2026, including weapons production, petrochemical zones, and Bushehr nuclear plant, while Iran confirmed shoot-downs of multiple U.S. aircraft and maintained threats of retaliation against regional and U.S. targets.
